title: June 2022 — 'always give, the take will come' (cater for other needs)
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-line June 2022 maxim: "Always give, the take will come" — supplemented with "cater for other needs."
  • A foundational give first belief that Yahir restates in many forms across the vault (e.g., the debt of gratitude frame, surface area of luck reasoning, the 2024 12 14 departing china reciprocity reflections).
  • Closely related to but distinct from dad wisdom — this one reads more as a Dale Carnegie / How to Win Friends echo than a Dad-quote.
  • "Cater for other needs" is the operational corollary: the way you give is by understanding what someone else actually wants, not by giving what you'd want.
  • One of the cleanest two-line manifestos in the 2022 corpus — reads like a manifesto style note with no hedging.

What's actually in the note

Always give, the take will come.
Cater for other needs

Why it matters

This is a concept-grade aphorism that connects multiple downstream nodes:

  1. give first as Yahir's interpersonal default. Underlies debt of gratitude (toward family) and the way 2024 12 14 departing china reflects on host-family reciprocity.
  2. dale carnegie echo. "Cater for other needs" is almost verbatim Win Friends — confirms Yahir was reading or absorbing that material by mid-2022.
  3. Counterweight to the 2022 mental health cluster. The same Yahir who got dizzy in financial-aid offices was also writing crisp generosity-as-strategy maxims a month later — the 2022 emotional landscape isn't monotone.

Open questions

  • Direct source? (No attribution; reads as Yahir's own phrasing of an absorbed principle.)
  • Operationalized where? (No follow-up "here's how I gave" notes — stays at the maxim level.)

Wikilinks

give first · debt of gratitude · surface area of luck · dale carnegie · dad wisdom · 2024 12 14 departing china · 2022 07 22 relax let thing flow · disconnected from outcome
